growth_records.medium: Chemically defined medium (CDM) with glucose and lactic acid

up.method: Monod affinity constant K_S; no volumetric uptake rate reported

sec.method: Main fermentation product (qualitative); no rate measured

growth_records.cite: Sangha JS et al. Advancing dental biofilm models: the integral role of pH in predicting S. mutans colonization. mSphere (2024). DOI:10.1128/msphere.00743-24. [PMC11774048]
growth_records.snippet: Streptococcus mutans Glucose 0.406 5.55 1.00 ... facultative anaerobes that are assumed to consume glucose (carbon source and electron donor) in anaerobic conditions and produce lactic acid as the main product
growth_records.notes: mu_max = 0.406 h-1 (Table 2), K_S = 5.55 mmol/L (=1.00 g/L glucose). Doubling time calculated: td = ln2/mu = 0.6931/0.406 = 1.707 h. Kinetic parameters CITED FROM Sangha et al. 2024, Microbiol Spectr, doi:10.1128/spectrum.03713-23 (ref 17). Modeling paper; no new measured qS/qP rates.

growth_records.cite: Chen, Yi-Ywan M et al. The GlnR Regulon in Streptococcus mutans Is Differentially Regulated by GlnR and PmrA. PLoS ONE (2016). DOI:10.1371/journal.pone.0159599. [PMC4959772]
growth_records.snippet: Strain generation time of 42 min in BHI (Fig 1B growth kinetics).
growth_records.notes: Batch BHI growth kinetics. Reported generation (doubling) time = 42 min = 0.7 h. mu=ln2/td=0.6931/0.7=0.99 /h. No uptake/secretion rates measured in this regulation study. WT GS5 stated 'similar to' delta-PmrA at 42 min; delta-GlnR & delta-GlnR_PmrA = 56 min.

growth_records.cite: Chen, Yi-Ywan M et al. The GlnR Regulon in Streptococcus mutans Is Differentially Regulated by GlnR and PmrA. PLoS ONE (2016). DOI:10.1371/journal.pone.0159599. [PMC4959772]
growth_records.snippet: Strain generation time of 56 min in BHI (Fig 1B growth kinetics).
growth_records.notes: Batch BHI growth kinetics. Reported generation (doubling) time = 56 min = 0.933 h. mu=ln2/td=0.6931/0.933=0.743 /h. No uptake/secretion rates measured in this regulation study. WT GS5 stated 'similar to' delta-PmrA at 42 min; delta-GlnR & delta-GlnR_PmrA = 56 min.

up.method: not quantified as a rate; qualitative: 20 mM glucose fully consumed (undetectable in filtrate) = growth-limiting

growth_records.cite: Chen, Yi-Ywan M et al. The GlnR Regulon in Streptococcus mutans Is Differentially Regulated by GlnR and PmrA. PLoS ONE (2016). DOI:10.1371/journal.pone.0159599. [PMC4959772]
growth_records.snippet: cultivated ... in TY medium (30 g tryptone and 5 g yeast extract liter-1) supplemented with 20 mM or 100 mM glucose at a dilution rate of 0.3 h-1 ... maintained at pH 7.2 and 5.5
growth_records.notes: Continuous chemostat, TY (tryptone 30 g/L, yeast extract 5 g/L) + 20 mM glucose, D=0.3 /h, pH 7.2, 37C, >=10 generations to steady state. mu=D=0.3 /h; td=ln2/0.3=2.31 h. Glucose status: 20 mM glucose fully consumed (undetectable in filtrate) = growth-limiting. No specific uptake/secretion rates reported (transcription-regulation study). At 20 mM glucose is growth-limiting (undetectable in filtrate); at 100 mM ~50 mM residual (excess).

up.method: Max acetaldehyde at 2000 mM ethanol; C1/2 = 478.5 +/- 70.0 mM

sec.method: Vmax by GC on resting washed cells at 2000 mM ethanol; +/- 3.5

growth_records.cite: Tagaino R et al. Bifacial biological effects of ethanol: acetaldehyde production by oral Streptococcus species and the antibacterial effects of ethanol against these bacteria. Journal of Oral Microbiology (2021). DOI:10.1080/20002297.2021.1937884. [PMC8204988]
growth_records.notes: Resting-cell assay. Acetaldehyde Vmax=25.7 umol/mg wet wt/min at 2000 mM ethanol; C1/2=478.5 mM. S. mutans tolerant to 0-500 mM ethanol (growth), inhibited at 1000 mM.
